title: engelmannia nuttalliana
intro: Engelmannia nuttalliana, also known as Engelmann's daisy or prairie daisy, is a flowering plant native to the central and western United States. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and as a diuretic. The plant contains flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, providing potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. Engelmannia nuttalliana can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in herbal supplements, making it a valuable resource in natural medicine.
